# Lives of Faith

> Christ-centred biographies of Christians throughout history — hymn writers, theologians, missionaries, martyrs, and believers from every era and region. Every story points to Christ and the gospel of salvation by grace through faith alone, not by works. Deliberately global in scope: includes Africa, Asia, Latin America, Eastern Europe, and the Global South, not only Britain and America.

For worship leaders, Bible teachers, and families seeking Christ-centred biographical content for services, devotions, and family worship. Each person has two versions of their story: a fuller "adult" version for worship/teaching use, and a simpler "family" version for children aged 8–14. All biographical facts are grounded in Wikipedia and cited sources — nothing is invented. Portrait images are AI-generated with no copyright claimed. Roman Catholics, Mormons, Jehovah's Witnesses, Christian Scientists, Word of Faith / prosperity gospel figures, Oneness Pentecostals, and other heterodox movements are excluded by design.

## Site

- [About & methodology](https://livesoffaith.org/about.html): purpose, sourcing, image process, human review system, and exclusions explained.
- [Memorial map](https://livesoffaith.org/map.html): interactive map of gravestones, statues, plaques, and monuments, with a heritage tour planner.
- [Full plain-text biographies](https://livesoffaith.org/llms-full.txt): every person's full story text in one file, for deep grounding.
- [Hymn stories](https://livesoffaith.org/hymns.html): the documented history behind individual hymns — who wrote them, when, and the Scripture that shaped them.
- [Timeline](https://livesoffaith.org/timeline.html): interactive lifespan comparison of every profiled person, set against major events in church and world history.

## People

- [A. W. Tozer (1897–1963)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=aw-tozer): Aiden Wilson Tozer (1897-1963) was an American pastor, editor, and devotional writer with the Christian and Missionary Alliance. Converted at seventeen in 1915, he entered…
- [Abraham Kuyper (1837–1920)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=abraham-kuyper): Abraham Kuyper (1837-1920) was a Dutch Reformed theologian, journalist, and statesman who served as Prime Minister of the Netherlands from 1901 to 1905. He shifted from liberal to…
- [Adoniram Judson (1788–1850)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=adoniram-judson): Adoniram Judson (1788–1850) was an American Baptist missionary, the first American Protestant foreign missionary, sent to Burma (Myanmar). He sailed for Burma with his wife Ann in…
- [Alexander Cruden (1699–1770)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=alexander-cruden): Alexander Cruden (1699–1770) was a Scottish Presbyterian bookseller, proofreader, and biblical scholar who lived and worked in London. He compiled his Complete Concordance to the…
- [Alexander Whyte (1836–1921)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=alexander-whyte): Alexander Whyte (1836–1921) was born illegitimate and raised in poverty in Kirriemuir, Scotland, apprenticed as a shoemaker before a local minister taught him Latin and Greek. He…
- [Allen Gardiner (1794–c. 1851)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=allen-gardiner): Allen Francis Gardiner (1794-1851) was a British Royal Navy officer who, after his first wife's death in 1834, turned to missionary work, founding a short-lived mission in Zulu…
- [Amy Carmichael (1867–1951)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=amy-carmichael): Amy Carmichael (1867–1951) was an Irish Protestant missionary to India who founded the Dohnavur Fellowship in Tamil Nadu. She arrived in India in 1895 and never returned to…
- [Andraé Crouch (1942–2015)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=andrae-crouch): Andraé Crouch (1942-2015) was an American gospel singer, songwriter, and Church of God in Christ pastor from Pacoima, California, widely called "the father of modern gospel music"…
- [Andrew Fuller (1754–1815)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=andrew-fuller): Andrew Fuller (1754–1815) was an English Particular Baptist minister and theologian who pastored Kettering Baptist Church from 1782. His 1785 work The Gospel Worthy of All…

- [Anthony Norris Groves (1795–1853)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=anthony-norris-groves): Anthony Norris Groves (1795–1853) was an English Protestant missionary, dentist, and a founding figure of the Plymouth Brethren movement, called the father of faith missions for…

- [Keith Green (1953–1982)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=keith-green): Keith Gordon Green (1953–1982) was an American Christian singer-songwriter and evangelist associated with the Jesus Movement. Born in Brooklyn to a Jewish family, he converted to…
- [Kenneth N. Taylor (1917–2005)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=kenneth-taylor): Kenneth N. Taylor (1917-2005) was an American editor and publisher best known as the creator of The Living Bible, a paraphrase of Scripture into contemporary English that he began…
- [Kristyn Getty (b. 1980)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=kristyn-getty): Kristyn Getty (b. 1980), née Lennox, is a Northern Irish hymn writer and vocalist born in Belfast and educated at Ballyclare High School. Introduced to Keith Getty in 2002 by her…
- [Leon Morris (1914–2006)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=leon-morris): Leon Lamb Morris (1914-2006) was an Australian Evangelical Anglican priest and New Testament scholar. Ordained in 1938, he earned a PhD at Cambridge and published The Apostolic…
- [Lilias Trotter (1853–1928)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=lilias-trotter): Isabella Lilias Trotter (1853–1928) was a British artist and missionary to Algeria. Identified as a gifted painter by art critic John Ruskin, who offered to train her personally,…
- [Lin Zhao 林昭 (1932–1968)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=lin-zhao): Lin Zhao (1932-1968), born Peng Lingzhao, was a Chinese Christian dissident executed during China's Cultural Revolution era for her writings opposing Mao's totalitarian rule.…
- [Lord Shaftesbury (1801–1885)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=lord-shaftesbury): Anthony Ashley Cooper, 7th Earl of Shaftesbury (1801–1885) was an English politician, philanthropist, and devout evangelical Anglican. He led Parliamentary campaigns for the…
- [Lottie Moon (1840–1912)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=lottie-moon): Charlotte 'Lottie' Digges Moon (1840–1912) was a Southern Baptist missionary to China. Born to a wealthy Virginia family, she was converted during a college revival led by John…
- [Louis Zamperini (1917–2014)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=louis-zamperini): Louis Zamperini (1917-2014) was an American distance runner who competed in the 5,000 metres at the 1936 Berlin Olympics. During World War II he served as a bombardier in the US…

- [Mary Slessor (1848–1915)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=mary-slessor): Mary Mitchell Slessor (1848–1915) was a Scottish missionary to Nigeria. Born in Aberdeen and raised in poverty in Dundee, where she worked in a jute mill from age eleven, she…
- [Matthew Henry (1662–1714)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=matthew-henry): Matthew Henry (1662–1714) was a Welsh-born Nonconformist minister and biblical expositor, son of Philip Henry, who was ejected from the Church of England under the Act of…

- [Mitsuo Fuchida (1902–1976)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=mitsuo-fuchida): Mitsuo Fuchida (1902-1976) was a Japanese Imperial Navy pilot who commanded the first wave of the Pearl Harbor attack on December 7, 1941, and was wounded at the Battle of Midway…
- [Nate Saint (1923–1956)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=nate-saint): Nate Saint (1923–1956) was an American missionary pilot with Mission Aviation Fellowship, based at Shell Mera, Ecuador. He pioneered the 'spiraling line' technique for lowering…

- [Son Yang-won 손양원 (1902–1950)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=son-yang-won): Son Yang-won (1902-1950) was a Korean Presbyterian pastor who ministered for decades among leprosy patients at Aeyangwon, Yeosu. Imprisoned by Japanese colonial authorities for…
- [Susanna Wesley (1669–1742)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=susanna-wesley): Susanna Wesley (1669–1742) was an English Anglican laywoman, the mother of John Wesley and Charles Wesley. She ran a disciplined Christian household at Epworth rectory in…
- [Thomas Barnardo (1845–1905)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=thomas-barnardo): Thomas John Barnardo (1845–1905) was an Irish-born Christian philanthropist and founder of homes for destitute children. Born in Dublin and converted through the Plymouth Brethren…
- [Thomas Cranmer (1489–1556)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=thomas-cranmer): Thomas Cranmer (1489-1556) was Archbishop of Canterbury and chief architect of the English Reformation, authoring the Book of Common Prayer. Imprisoned by Mary I, he recanted his…
- [Thomas Watson (c. 1620–1686)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=thomas-watson): Thomas Watson (c. 1620–1686) was an English Puritan minister and theologian. Educated at Emmanuel College, Cambridge, he served as rector of St Stephen Walbrook in the City of…
- [Tiyo Soga (c. 1829–1871)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=tiyo-soga): Tiyo Soga (1829–1871) was a Xhosa South African who became the first black South African ordained as a Christian minister, trained in the United Presbyterian Church of Scotland.…

- [William Carey (1761–1834)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=william-carey): William Carey (1761–1834) was an English Baptist minister and missionary known as the father of modern missions. A self-educated cobbler from Northamptonshire, he wrote An Enquiry…
- [William Cowper (1731–1800)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=william-cowper): William Cowper (1731–1800) was an English poet and hymn writer who co-produced the Olney Hymns (1779) with John Newton. He suffered severe and recurring mental illness throughout…
- [William Herbert Brewster Sr. (1897–1987)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=william-herbert-brewster): William Herbert Brewster Sr. (1897-1987) was a Baptist pastor in Memphis, Tennessee, and a pioneering gospel songwriter who composed over 200 songs and staged early African…
- [William MacDonald (1917–2007)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=william-macdonald): William MacDonald (1917–2007) was an American Plymouth Brethren teacher and author. He served as president of Emmaus Bible College in Oak Park, Illinois (1959–1965) before…
- [William Tyndale (c. 1494–1536)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=william-tyndale): William Tyndale (c.1494–1536) was an English scholar and Bible translator who produced the first printed English New Testament translated from the original Greek. Refused official…
- [William Wilberforce (1759–1833)](https://livesoffaith.org/person.html?id=william-wilberforce): William Wilberforce (1759–1833) was an English politician and evangelical Anglican who led the parliamentary campaign to abolish the British slave trade. He underwent evangelical…

## Hymn Stories

- [A Mighty Fortress Is Our God (c. 1527-1529)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=a-mighty-fortress-is-our-god): written by Martin Luther, grounded in Psalm 46.
- [Alas! and Did My Savior Bleed (1707)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=alas-and-did-my-savior-bleed): written by Isaac Watts, grounded in Romans 6.
- [All Things Bright and Beautiful (1848)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=all-things-bright-and-beautiful): written by Cecil Frances Alexander, grounded in Psalm 104:24-25.
- [All the Way My Savior Leads Me (1875)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=all-the-way-my-savior-leads-me): written by Fanny Crosby, grounded in Psalm 23.
- [Almost Persuaded (1871)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=almost-persuaded): written by Philip Bliss, grounded in Acts 26:28.
- [Amazing Grace (1772/1773)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=amazing-grace): written by John Newton, grounded in 1 Chronicles 17:16-17.
- [Amazing Love (And Can It Be) (1738)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=amazing-love): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in Acts 12:6-11.
- [Angels from the Realms of Glory (1816)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=angels-from-the-realms-of-glory): written by James Montgomery, grounded in Luke 2:8-14.
- [Beauty for Brokenness (God of the Poor) (1993)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=beauty-for-brokenness): written by Graham Kendrick, grounded in Isaiah 61:1-3.
- [Blessed Assurance (1873)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=blessed-assurance): written by Fanny Crosby, grounded in Romans 8:16.
- [By Gracious Powers (December 1944)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=by-gracious-powers): written by Dietrich Bonhoeffer, grounded in Psalm 121:7-8.
- [Christ Arose! (Low in the Grave He Lay) (1874)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=christ-arose): written by Robert Lowry, grounded in Matthew 28:6.
- [Christ the Lord Is Risen Today (1739)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=christ-the-lord-is-risen-today): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in 1 Corinthians 15.
- [Come, Thou Long-Expected Jesus (1744)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=come-thou-long-expected-jesus): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in Haggai 2:7.
- [Give Me Jesus (1879)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=give-me-jesus): written by Fanny Crosby, grounded in Philippians 3:8.
- [Glorious Things of Thee Are Spoken (1779)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=glorious-things-of-thee-are-spoken): written by John Newton, grounded in Psalm 87.
- [God Moves in a Mysterious Way (1773)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=god-moves-in-a-mysterious-way): written by William Cowper, grounded in Romans 11:33.
- [Hail to the Lord's Anointed (1821)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=hail-to-the-lords-anointed): written by James Montgomery, grounded in Psalm 72.
- [Hark the Glad Sound! (c. 1735 (posthumous 1755))](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=hark-the-glad-sound): written by Philip Doddridge, grounded in Luke 4:18-19.
- [Hark, the Herald Angels Sing (1739)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=hark-the-herald-angels-sing): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in Luke 2.
- [He Looked Beyond My Fault and Saw My Need (1968)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=he-looked-beyond-my-fault-and-saw-my-need): written by Dottie Rambo, grounded in Romans 5:8.
- [I Bind Unto Myself Today (St Patrick's Breastplate) (1889)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=i-bind-unto-myself-today): written by Cecil Frances Alexander, grounded in Ephesians 6:10-18.
- [I Know That My Redeemer Lives (1775)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=i-know-that-my-redeemer-lives): written by Samuel Medley, grounded in Job 19:25.
- [I Know Whom I Have Believed (1883)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=i-know-whom-i-have-believed): written by Daniel W. Whittle, grounded in 2 Timothy 1:12.
- [I Need Thee Every Hour (1872)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=i-need-thee-every-hour): written by Robert Lowry, grounded in John 15:5.
- [I'd Rather Have Jesus (1922 poem / 1932 testimony)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=id-rather-have-jesus): written by George Beverly Shea, grounded in Mark 8:36.
- [I'll Overcome Someday (1901)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=ill-overcome-someday): written by Charles Albert Tindley, grounded in 1 John 5:4.
- [In Christ Alone (2001)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=in-christ-alone): written by Keith Getty, grounded in Romans 3:25-26.
- [It Is Well with My Soul (1873)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=it-is-well-with-my-soul): written by Horatio Spafford, grounded in Colossians 2:14.
- [Jesus Shall Reign (1719)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=jesus-shall-reign): written by Isaac Watts, grounded in Psalm 72.
- [Joy to the World (1719)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=joy-to-the-world): written by Isaac Watts, grounded in Psalm 98.
- [Just As I Am, Without One Plea (1835)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=just-as-i-am-without-one-plea): written by Charlotte Elliott, grounded in John 6:37.
- [Knowing You (All I Once Held Dear) (1993)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=knowing-you): written by Graham Kendrick, grounded in Philippians 3:7-11.
- [Let the Lower Lights Be Burning (1871)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=let-the-lower-lights-be-burning): written by Philip Bliss, grounded in Matthew 5:14-16.
- [Lizalis' idinga lakho (Fulfil Your Promise) (July 1857)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=lizalise-idinga-lakho): written by Tiyo Soga, grounded in 2 Corinthians 1:20.
- [Love Divine, All Loves Excelling (1747)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=love-divine-all-loves-excelling): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in 2 Corinthians 3:18.
- [Man of Sorrows What a Name (1875)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=man-of-sorrows-what-a-name): written by Philip Bliss, grounded in Isaiah 53:3.
- [Marching to Zion (1867)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=marching-to-zion): written by Robert Lowry, grounded in Isaiah 35:10.
- [Meekness and Majesty (1986)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=meekness-and-majesty): written by Graham Kendrick, grounded in Philippians 2:6-11.
- [Move On Up a Little Higher (1946)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=move-on-up-a-little-higher): written by William Herbert Brewster Sr., grounded in Philippians 3:14.
- [O God of Bethel (c. 1736 (posthumous 1755; revised 1781))](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=o-god-of-bethel): written by Philip Doddridge, grounded in Genesis 28:20-22.
- [O God, Our Help in Ages Past (1708/1719)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=o-god-our-help-in-ages-past): written by Isaac Watts, grounded in Psalm 90.
- [O Happy Day (c. 1755 (posthumous))](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=o-happy-day): written by Philip Doddridge, grounded in Acts 8:35-39.
- [O Love That Wilt Not Let Me Go (1882)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=o-love-that-wilt-not-let-me-go): written by George Matheson, grounded in Romans 8:38-39.
- [O for a Closer Walk with God (1769)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=o-for-a-closer-walk-with-god): written by William Cowper, grounded in Genesis 5:24.
- [Oh Lord, You're Beautiful (1980)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=oh-lord-youre-beautiful): written by Keith Green, grounded in Psalm 27:4.
- [Oh, for a Thousand Tongues to Sing (1739)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=oh-for-a-thousand-tongues-to-sing): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in Psalm 34:1-3.
- [Once in Royal David's City (1848)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=once-in-royal-davids-city): written by Cecil Frances Alexander, grounded in Luke 2:4-7.
- [Rejoice, the Lord Is King (1746)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=rejoice-the-lord-is-king): written by Charles Wesley, grounded in Philippians 4:4.
- [Rescue the Perishing (1869)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=rescue-the-perishing): written by Fanny Crosby, grounded in Jude 1:23.
- [Safe in the Arms of Jesus (1868)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=safe-in-the-arms-of-jesus): written by Fanny Crosby, grounded in Deuteronomy 33:27.
- [Shall We Gather at the River (1864)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=shall-we-gather-at-the-river): written by Robert Lowry, grounded in Revelation 22:1.
- [Shine, Jesus, Shine (1987)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=shine-jesus-shine): written by Graham Kendrick, grounded in 1 Timothy 6:16.
- [Surely, God Is Able (c. 1949-1950)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=surely-god-is-able): written by William Herbert Brewster Sr., grounded in Daniel 3.
- [Take My Life, and Let It Be (1874)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=take-my-life-and-let-it-be): written by Frances Ridley Havergal, grounded in Romans 12:1.
- [The Ninety and Nine (1874)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=the-ninety-and-nine): written by Ira Sankey, grounded in Luke 15:3-7.
- [The Power of the Cross (2005)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=the-power-of-the-cross): written by Keith Getty, grounded in Isaiah 53:4-6.
- [The Servant King (1983)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=the-servant-king): written by Graham Kendrick, grounded in Philippians 2:5-8.
- [The Wonder of It All (1955)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=the-wonder-of-it-all): written by George Beverly Shea, grounded in 1 John 3:1.
- [There Is a Fountain Filled with Blood (1772)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=there-is-a-fountain-filled-with-blood): written by William Cowper, grounded in Zechariah 13:1.
- [There Is a Green Hill Far Away (1848)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=there-is-a-green-hill-far-away): written by Cecil Frances Alexander, grounded in Isaiah 53:5-6.
- [There Is a Higher Throne (2003)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=there-is-a-higher-throne): written by Kristyn Getty, grounded in Revelation 7:9-10.
- [To God Be the Glory (1875)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=to-god-be-the-glory): written by Fanny Crosby, grounded in John 3:16.
- [What a Friend We Have in Jesus (1855)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=what-a-friend-we-have-in-jesus): written by Joseph M. Scriven, grounded in John 15:14-15.
- [When I Survey the Wondrous Cross (1707)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=when-i-survey-the-wondrous-cross): written by Isaac Watts, grounded in Galatians 6:14.
- [Your Love Broke Through (1977)](https://livesoffaith.org/hymn.html?id=your-love-broke-through): written by Keith Green, grounded in Luke 15:4-6.
